disclaimer: Launch-development pricing. DDG sells budgeted application/model-router output, skill/workflow/MCP artifacts, and agent-run results, not provider credentials, account seats, auth tokens, or raw account access. Account/subscription-backed model execution remains internal/manual beta or contract-only unless provider terms explicitly allow multi-tenant service use.

sources.name: Kimi Code CLI / K2.7 Code plan evidence

services.description: Automated website audit for agents evaluating local-business opportunities. Intro price lowered for settlement testing.

One-cent receipt-only payment smoke-test endpoint for AI agents to prove their own x402/direct-crypto/payment-client flow can complete a DDG paid request. This is infrastructure validation, not a meaningful revenue product, and has no external side effect beyond payment verification/settlement.

Live OpenAI-compatible paid model-router completion endpoint for AI agents. Supports conversation, analysis, code/help prompts, DDG routing extensions, payment gating, budget caps, and receipts. Eligible launch model prices target 75% of comparable official API list pricing; no provider-key/account exposure.

Live bounded agent-task endpoint. Local runtime routes are free for a limited time and can be selected across Ollama, llama.cpp, and LM Studio/llmster when the requested model is listed healthy under that runtime. Use route prefixes such as ollama/<model>, llama.cpp/<model>, lm_studio/<model>, or provide model plus runtime. Provider-backed Kimi K2.7 Code and Claude artifact routes remain paid/env-gated/manual-approved and never expose raw credentials/tokens/seats.
services.seat_policy: one concurrent free local generation seat per healthy local runtime endpoint (Ollama, llama.cpp, or LM Studio/llmster); default cap 2 for a two configured 8GB-VRAM runtime backends; 24-hour per-agent lease, 100 requests/lease, 60s swarm generation timeout, 120s agent-run timeout; reusable after lease expiry

services.description: Operator-reviewed DDG-operated model/artifact run using available backend capacity, including account/API/subscription-backed coding tools where technically and commercially eligible. Buyers receive bounded outputs/artifacts only; DDG never transfers auth tokens, account seats, raw credentials, or provider sessions. Launch pricing targets 75% of comparable official API list pricing where applicable.
Paid order-intake endpoint for all manual/beta DDG agent offerings. Records a redacted order queue item after payment and requires operator review before fulfillment or any irreversible action.
Human-reviewed audit with stronger client-facing recommendations.
Small curated local-business lead pack with evidence URLs and outreach angles.
Browser-based proof/QA capture with screenshots and reproducible steps.
Personalized outreach brief/draft. Does not send messages without human approval.
Create or repair llms.txt, OpenAPI, pricing/catalog JSON, and agent-readable service docs.
Audit an MCP server or tool surface for installability, schema quality, auth, and prompt-injection risk.
Probe an agent-facing website/API/MCP/tool for prompt injection, data exfiltration, unsafe tool-use, and mitigation notes.
Turn a repo/docs/SOP corpus into an agent-ready context pack, skill, workflow, or implementation guide.
Repair OpenAPI/MCP/tool schemas so agents can reliably call them, with examples and validation.
Paid source-linked research brief with citations, confidence notes, and launch recommendation.
Compare a current/proposed page and return conversion, trust, copy, and agent-readability improvements.
Create a private lead-specific prototype/demo concept for a local business, with evidence and staged offer angle.
Audit an API/service for x402/MPP/402 paid-agent readiness, idempotency, receipts, and discovery assets.
Run a bounded CLI-agent/model task in a sandbox and return the artifact/report, never provider credentials.
Build a Hermes skill, generic agent skill, or reusable workflow for the buyer's existing AI/agent stack.
Use DDG-operated models to convert a procedure into a skill/workflow usable by Claude, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode, Hermes, or another agent.
Design and scaffold an MCP tool/server from a plain-English goal, including schema, install docs, and smoke tests.

Named paid lease remains planned; the current live local free-seat pool is exposed through /v1/micro-model-swarm-preview and local Ollama routes under /v1/model/agent-run. Candidate menu includes Qwen/Qwen-derived coder, abliterated/less-restrictive research models, and other GGUF/Ollama routes behind payment edge policy, usage caps, and abuse controls.

Live free/best-effort local model swarm preview with strict identity, lease, request, and local-runtime seat caps. Agents may select Ollama, llama.cpp, or LM Studio/llmster when the model is listed healthy for that runtime; combos default to Ollama unless a compatible runtime/model is requested.
services.seat_policy: one concurrent free local generation seat per healthy local runtime endpoint (Ollama, llama.cpp, or LM Studio/llmster); default cap 2 for a two configured 8GB-VRAM runtime backends; 24-hour per-agent lease, 100 requests/lease, 60s swarm generation timeout, 120s agent-run timeout; reusable after lease expiry

services.description: Free launch-beta read-only Ethereum full-node query endpoint behind the DDG AI-agent edge. It is implemented with method whitelist, sync readiness gate, fair-use lease timer, per-agent request caps, and upstream max-concurrency cap; public usefulness starts after overflow runtime backend Reth execution sync completes.

free_policy.sync_gate: DDG_ETH_RPC_REQUIRE_SYNCED defaults true; returns 503 reth_rpc_syncing until eth_syncing=false and latest block is non-zero
free_policy.safety: read-only method whitelist; blocks eth_sendRawTransaction/admin/debug/personal; raw Reth RPC must stay private behind DDG edge

services.description: Lists currently available local-runtime models for Ollama plus healthy OpenAI-compatible llama.cpp and LM Studio/llmster servers, free-seat policy, lease/request caps, and requestable local runtimes. Public requests never trigger model downloads or expose raw LAN endpoints.
services.seat_policy: one concurrent free local generation seat per healthy local runtime endpoint (Ollama, llama.cpp, or LM Studio/llmster); default cap 2 for a two configured 8GB-VRAM runtime backends; 24-hour per-agent lease, 100 requests/lease, 60s swarm generation timeout, 120s agent-run timeout; reusable after lease expiry

services.description: AI agents can request a new local model/runtime (Ollama, llama.cpp GGUF, LM Studio/llmster, OpenAI-compatible local server, or vLLM). Public requests never auto-download; DDG checks disk/safety/license, binding/auth posture, reserve/max-size caps, downloads manually, smokes, then marks READY.

Free static AI skill safety scanner for prompt-injection, credential-exfiltration, dangerous tool-use, package lifecycle, install-script, and broad file/network access red flags. Best-effort/no-SLA; raw secret-like values are redacted.

Kimi K2.7 Code is available as a DDG-operated paid artifact/result route and can be used within Kimi Code. Buyers receive bounded outputs/artifacts only; DDG never transfers provider credentials, account seats, or raw provider sessions.
Prompt-injection and tool-exfiltration scan: risk_summary, exploit_reproduction_steps, prompt/tool-boundary mitigations.
npm/package-lock dependency risk scan: known_vulnerabilities, maintainer/supply-chain risk signals, install-script risk.
Python dependency and import-risk scan: known_vulnerabilities, dependency confusion risk, setup/install hooks.
API-key/secret leak and credential boundary scan: redacted_secret_findings, leak_paths, rotation_priority.
Malware/suspicious-code triage for agent workflows: suspicious_behaviors, network/file/process indicators, safe containment notes.
MCP/tool permission and auth-boundary audit: tool-risk matrix, dangerous capability flags, prompt-injection exposure.
Agent swarm supply-chain baseline bundle: combined prompt/package/secret/MCP findings, prioritized remediation plan, CI scanner starter config.

Productionized public-surface security audit for AI-agent services, MCP/tool servers, OpenAPI/llms.txt catalogs, dependency manifests, prompt-injection exposure, public endpoints, and payment/check-out safety. Ordered by AI agents through /v1/order-intake; operator-reviewed before fulfillment.

Agent checkout conformance audit for machine-payable services: validates 403->402->200->receipt flow, protocol advertisement, spend caps, idempotency replay, fake-token fail-closed behavior, and receipt/artifact semantics.

MPP/payment-rail onboarding package for AI-agent sellers: verifies provider/currency configuration, sidecar health, 402 protocol ordering, penny smoke path, replay protection, fake-proof rejection, and go-live checklist.

Buyer-facing artifact and receipt console pack for paid agent services. Base order status/artifact URLs are now live for every /v1/order-intake order; this paid pack adds a richer app/table/component spec, receipt schema, budget/rail metadata, audit trail, download links, and agent-readable status JSON.
Bundled launch package for AI-agent buyers and sellers: security_preflight, checkout_conformance, agent_readability_upgrade, mcp_tool_audit, and local_model_runtime_setup scoped into one paid order.

Create/polish an AI-agent service distribution packet: /.well-known/ai, API linkset, agent-skills index, agents.json, llms.txt/OpenAPI/catalog alignment, sitemap/robots, and registry/marketplace listing copy for MCP/x402/agent directories.

Prepare agent-marketplace submissions and PR/issue copy for x402 directories, MCP registries, agent-ready scanners, GitHub README/listing pages, and search-indexable service docs. No spam or unauthorized posting; operator approval required before external submission.

Score a domain/API for agent readiness: discovery endpoints, OpenAPI, llms.txt, agent-skill index, API catalog linkset, payment 402 behavior, auth docs, MCP status, sitemap/robots, and machine-readable commerce signals.

Run a DDG buyer-agent simulation against a target service: discover capabilities, verify 403/402/payment challenge behavior, fake-payment fail-closed, idempotency, and artifact/status usability. Spending tests require explicit operator-approved credentials/budget.

model_router.positioning: Paid AI-agent-queryable model router / inference concierge. Buyers pay DDG for budgeted model outputs/results; DDG routes across available legally/technically eligible backend capacity, including local Ollama workers and DDG-operated account/API/artifact workflows. Public docs never sell or transfer raw provider credentials, auth sessions, account seats, or tokens.
pricing_notes: Launch rates for eligible AI-agent model query/artifact routes are 75% of comparable official API list pricing where DDG can fulfill through available backend capacity.
DDG sells model outputs/results and bounded artifact runs, not raw Claude/OpenAI/Codex/Cursor/Kimi provider credentials, sessions, account seats, or tokens.

Account/subscription-backed coding capacity may be used only as DDG-operated backend capacity with operator controls and no buyer credential exposure; keep public docs framed as result/artifact delivery.
Official API/commercial-contract rails remain the cleanest public self-serve path; account-backed routes stay manual/operator-reviewed unless provider terms explicitly allow broader service use.
All model routes require AI-agent identity, payment gating, spend caps, and receipts before production advertising.
Limited-time local model promo: all local Ollama/llama.cpp/LM Studio runtime options are free/best-effort/no-SLA while capacity allows.
Post-promo local model policy: only models 4B and smaller remain free; >4B local models become paid or operator-approved capacity.
Public model-download requests are queue-only and must preserve disk reserve; they never trigger automatic ollama pull or GGUF download.
safe_packaging: OpenAI-compatible request body with DDG routing policy extensions
Budget caps per call and per buyer
Exact upstream identity shown only for explicitly supported/allowed models
Human approval for risky tool/spend side effects

models.notes: Lightweight cheap route; user asked for Air-style GLM. Official docs show GLM-4.5-Air, not GLM-4.6-Air.

Z.AI llms.txt positions GLM-5.2 for Coding Plan usage; DDG provisional launch validation price matches 75% of GLM-5.1 until Z.AI publishes separate public token pricing. Requires funded GLM account/resource package before live use.

models.provider_reference: GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark / GPT-5.3-Codex naming varies by surface
models.notes: Only after terms/API availability verification; OpenAI Codex docs say Spark research preview for ChatGPT Pro users only at launch.
API-backed only. Claude Code Pro/Max OAuth must not be routed for third-party buyers.
Official current docs show Sonnet 4.6, not Sonnet 4.7. Claude Code Pro/Max OAuth must not be routed for third-party buyers.
Most capable Claude route; manual cap recommended. Claude Code Pro/Max OAuth must not be routed for third-party buyers.
OpenAI Codex/ChatGPT subscription-backed relay is not enabled for public multi-tenant sale; enable only on API/contract rails.
OpenAI Codex/ChatGPT subscription-backed relay is not enabled for public multi-tenant sale; enable only on API/contract rails.
Router picks model from budget/policy. Default current route: glm-5-turbo; cheap route: glm-4.5-air.
Kimi K2.7 Code is available as a DDG-operated paid artifact/result route and can be used within Kimi Code. Env-gated/manual-approved; no raw credential/account/API resale.

account_local_candidate_routes.notes: Kimi K2.7 Code is available as a DDG-operated paid artifact/result route and can be used within Kimi Code. DDG returns bounded artifacts/results, not raw credential/account/API resale.
Claude Code execution is useful internally; public third-party routing remains disallowed under current Claude Code legal notes.

runtime_contract.public_account_route_policy: No raw account/auth/API-key resale; account/local routes must be DDG-operated artifact execution with sandboxing and provider permission.

account_backed_agent_routes.notes: Kimi K2.7 Code is available as DDG-operated backend capacity and can be used within Kimi Code; keep env-gated/operator-controlled unless provider terms permit broader self-serve.

launch_discount_policy.summary: Launch policy: eligible DDG-operated AI-agent model query/artifact routes are priced at 75% of the comparable official API list price where DDG can fulfill through available backend capacity. This is a DDG promotional service price, not resale of raw provider accounts.
launch_discount_policy.formula: ddg_price = official_api_list_price * 0.75 for eligible numeric per-token list prices
launch_discount_policy.credential_policy: No raw OAuth/account/session/token access is advertised, sold, leased, or transferred; buyers receive outputs/artifacts only.
launch_discount_policy.eligibility: Only routes DDG can operate technically and commercially with budget caps, logging, and operator controls. Manual/operator review remains required for account-backed coding/artifact workflows.

local_model_free_policy.current_promo: All local model runtime options are free for a limited time, best-effort, no SLA, identity required.
local_model_free_policy.future_free_tier: After the limited-time promo, only models 4B and smaller remain free.
local_model_free_policy.larger_models_after_promo: Models over 4B become paid or operator-approved local capacity after the promo.

local_model_download_policy.preferred_large_model_host: durable runtime backend first; keep overflow runtime backend storage for Reth sync, OS, and operator workspace.
local_model_download_policy.operator_review: Requests queue only. Operator checks license/safety/disk, downloads manually, smokes, then marks READY.

local_runtime_options.notes: Primary live local runtime behind /v1/micro-model-swarm-preview and free-promo /v1/model/agent-run local routes.
GGUF request path is supported; install/serve after operator approval and smoke.
LM Studio local server is a requestable runtime for users who prefer it over Ollama; not currently installed on overflow runtime backend/durable runtime backend.

free_local_model_agent_slots.claim: Connect these to your AI agent via DDG free local-model slots.
free_local_model_agent_slots.future_free_policy: After the promo, <=4B local models remain free; >4B local routes become paid/operator-approved capacity.

supported_local_runtimes.notes: Production free-slot runtime; dynamic downloaded-model catalog.
GGUF-native runtime target for the same model files; expose exact repo/file identity.
LM Studio requires desktop/local server support; keep as operator-request until a headless-compatible service is verified.
Any local runtime exposing OpenAI-compatible /v1/chat/completions can be added behind the same payment/free-slot edge.

free_local_model_agent_slots.context_disclosure: Catalog lists native GGUF context and DDG service default context separately. Native context is model metadata; DDG free slots default to 32K tokens for large GGUF aliases unless a route states otherwise.

anchor_models.notes: abliterated Qwen3 8B route on durable runtime backend
Dolphin3 abliterated durable runtime backend route
long-context small Mistral route on durable runtime backend

priority_ab_rollout_models.notes: GLM-family abliterated route; IQ3 chosen for 8GB-class hosts.

guardrails: Never sell or transfer provider API keys, auth tokens, account seats, or raw account access.
Do not route third-party buyer requests through Claude Free/Pro/Max credentials; Claude Code legal docs explicitly disallow this for third-party developers.
Treat ChatGPT/Codex, Cursor Pro, Z.ai Code, and Kimi Code subscription-plan capacity as internal-only until explicit commercial permission exists.
Use official API/commercial rails for public model-router traffic; label non-enabled candidate routes separately from active aliases.
Block requests that try to train competing models on provider output where prohibited.
Add per-buyer quotas, abuse detection, logs, idempotency, and refund path before public launch.

subscription_backed_route_policy.allowed_private_use: DDG internal/manual beta tasks only; no provider credential exposure; no buyer claim that they are receiving account access.
yellow_routes: cursor-cloud-agent-api/composer-2-or-2.5 with explicit Cursor commercial approval
Kimi K2.7 Code provider-plan route with explicit Moonshot approval
green_routes: official APIs and commercial contracts: Kimi official API/commercial contract, Z.ai API, Anthropic API, OpenAI API

candidate_models_not_publicly_enabled.notes: Official Cursor model page exists; headless/Cloud Agent automation appears technically possible, but Pro account pooling is high-risk.
Official docs mention Auto + Composer 2.5 usage pool/API usage; do not advertise as public DDG route until commercial terms are clear.
Installed locally and authenticated as Claude Pro, but Claude docs explicitly forbid routing Free/Pro/Max credentials on behalf of users.
Official Kimi API route is not the current DDG launch direction; DDG is using Kimi K2.7 Code bounded artifacts instead of raw Kimi API proxying.

local_model_offer.gpu_observed: overflow runtime backend: 8GB-VRAM GPU, 535GiB free on Linux root while Reth is syncing and already ~981GiB. durable runtime backend: 8GB-VRAM GPU, ~1.9TiB free on root LV, Ollama 0.30.5. Prefer durable runtime backend for model-library expansion; avoid large overflow runtime backend pulls until Reth storage headroom is resolved.
local_model_offer.capacity_decision: Local runtime options (Ollama, llama.cpp, LM Studio/llmster) are free for a limited time as best-effort/no-SLA lead magnet capacity when the runtime/model is healthy. After promo, only <=4B local models remain free; larger local models move to paid/operator-approved capacity.
future_options_not_enabled_now: additional GGUF/model pulls after operator disk review

LM Studio/llmster local server option via OpenAI-compatible /v1/models and /v1/chat/completions behind the DDG edge.

two_host_production_target.primary-host: 8GB-VRAM GPU, production GPU seat when online
two_host_production_target.condition: both hosts must be configured in the internal backend pool and pass /api/tags health checks; raw Ollama URLs remain private behind payment edge

micro_model_swarm.capacity_policy: free local-runtime preview; buyer chooses one listed model, combo=auto, or predefined role combo; runtime can be ollama, llama.cpp, or lm_studio when that model is healthy/listed; strict lease/request/runtime/context/output caps; best-effort/no SLA.

ollama_candidates.recommended_role: tiny instruction/checker; license review before commercial use
not available in Ollama library under tested tag; evaluate separately via HF/llama.cpp

statuses.live_runtime: Implemented in the payment edge today and safe to advertise as a live HTTPS route, subject to beta payment and Cloudflare caveats.
statuses.manual_fulfillment_beta: Catalog offer is available only through DDG/operator review or manual fulfillment; do not advertise as an OpenAPI self-serve endpoint yet.
statuses.planned: Not public self-serve. Kept in the catalog as roadmap/packaging until wrappers, sandboxing, payment settlement, and smoke tests pass.

offer_contract.openapi_policy: OpenAPI advertises live_runtime paths and may include explicitly beta/503-until-ready paths only when runtime handlers exist and docs state availability limits. Manual offers stay in pricing.json and llms.txt until handlers and smoke tests exist.
offer_contract.payment_posture: Mock payments are for local/staging smoke only. Public production requires real x402/MPP settlement or explicit manual-payment beta language.
offer_contract.agent_run_posture: Live beta for allowlisted local Ollama artifact execution. Account/subscription-backed model runs are DDG-operated artifact execution only and remain environment-gated/manual-approved until sandboxing, provider terms, and settlement checks pass.

consumer_policy: AI-agents only. Requests without agent identity are rejected. DDG sells operated artifacts/results and metered service output, not raw credentials, account seats, or transferable auth tokens.

payment_posture_2026_06_06.coinbase_cdp: deferred; no Coinbase production dependency for initial launch because CDP x402 requires authenticated API keys and returned 401 without them
payment_posture_2026_06_06.x402_public_testnet: verify endpoint configured/tested for invalid-proof rejection; settle adapter implemented and needs a real buyer payload for end-to-end settlement smoke
payment_posture_2026_06_06.mpp: seller-side verifier sidecar scaffolded under edge/mpp_verifier_sidecar.mjs; requires mppx install plus Tempo/MPP recipient/currency env
payment_posture_2026_06_06.direct_crypto_manual: initial beta fallback: publish configured EVM/BTC/LTC/XMR/ZEC/DOGE addresses in 402 body, collect tx proof hash in order-intake, operator confirms before fulfillment

direct_crypto_manual.security: raw txid/proof is not written to order queue; only payment_proof_hash is stored; fulfillment waits for operator confirmation

rate_limits.key: path + agent identity hash or forwarded-for fallback

operator_notes: durable runtime backend an internal host is internally reachable and has Docker/Ollama running, but NVIDIA driver is blocked by nouveau until manual reboot after blacklist/initramfs update; visible GPUs are 8GB-VRAM GPU + a secondary GPU, not 8GB-VRAM GPU.

penny_tx_smoke_test.purpose: Let other AI agents smoke-test their x402/MPP/direct-crypto payment ability with the smallest safe DDG charge and a receipt-only response.
penny_tx_smoke_test.no_external_side_effect: The endpoint does not perform a separate blockchain transfer or irreversible action beyond verifying/settling the payment rail used to call it.

ethereum_reth_private_rpc.host_plan: overflow runtime backend first because it has ample NVMe storage; keep durable runtime backend focused on paid agent services and Ollama/payment edge.
ethereum_reth_private_rpc.account_policy: No third-party RPC vendor account required for buyers; DDG runs the node/proxy. Still AI-agent identity/payment gated at the DDG edge.
ethereum_reth_private_rpc.allowed_method_posture: Read-only method whitelist; block admin/miner/personal/debug/trace/sendRawTransaction by default.

local_model_expansion_candidates_2026_06.why: 12B abliterated/uncensored Gemma-family baseline; good mid-size refusal-reduced test.
Dolphin/Venice-style 24B general uncensored-ish candidate; durable runtime backend only.
Sparse/active-parameter coder abliterated route; useful if it smokes acceptably on 8GB+CPU hybrid.
Large Qwen3.6 uncensored/heretic option; durable runtime backend storage, experimental speed.
Large abliterated MoE-style experiment; request-only due speed/license/disk.
